title: The One Where Ross Can't Flirt

lines.text: Chandler and Joey's, Chandler is getting ready for a date with Monica as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! You look nice. What's the occasion?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica and I are celebrating our ten-month anniversary, we've got reservations at Ja George.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! How'd you get in there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Made a few calls, pulled some strings, and they agreed to seat us at 11:30 if we both had the chicken and didn't get desert.
There's a knock on the door and Chandler answers it. It's the pizza delivery girl.

lines.speaker: Delivery Girl
lines.text: Hi Chandler!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey Caitlin! Somebody got a haircut.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Ugh, I hate it! I look like an 8-year-old boy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, if that was true, gym class would've been a lot more interesting.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: It's uh, 27 dollars even.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay. (Hands her the money.) Here you go.
The duck starts quacking.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Hey, where's the chicken?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, he's in the back. The duck pissed him off, said that eggs came first.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: (laughs) Great. I'll see you later!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, bye!
He closes the door and turns to see Ross glaring at him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What the hell was that?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The flirting! Aren't you supposed to be going out with, I don't know hmm, let's say my sister?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was not flirting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It was totally flirting. "Somebody got a haircut (Makes some whiney, nasally noises.)"

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay first of all, the impression, uncanny. And second, that was not flirting, that was just casual conversation between two people. That is all.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, right.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You wanna see flirting? I'll show you flirting. (Starts to move towards Ross.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (backing away) I'm good.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel and Phoebe are in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am so proud of Joey, I can't believe he's going to be on Law & Order!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know. But don't you think that it should be called Order and Law?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No because first they arrest the guy and then they try him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Don't get me started on that.
Ross and Chandler enter with the pizzas.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was not flirting.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And on your anniversary, for shame!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler was hitting on the hot delivery girl!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was not and oh God, shh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well I'm sorry but you were! Okay? And besides if anyone should be hitting on her it's the guy who's single, the guy that who-who-who can do something about it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sounds like somebody wants to be Mr. Pizza Delivery Girl.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is that what this is about? You like Caitlin?
Ross looks at Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross! We broke up two years ago; you've been married since then. I think it's okay that we see other people.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I-I was watching her the other day at the pizza place.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And she's just so sexy and funny and has the cutest little...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, y'know what? We don't need her measurements.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (looking through the pizzas) Okay pepperoni, pepperoni, pepperoni, okay Ross, I know she's pretty and you love her, but is she stupid?! She forgot my vegetarian!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is perfect! She'll have to come back here with your pizza, and when she does, I'll turn on the Charm-O-Ross. Oh I'm so glad you don't eat meat.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See? Vegetarianism benefits everyone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering, with his grandmother) Hey everybody, look who's here! You remember my grandmother!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Big night!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is so cool!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, Joey on Law & Order, you must be very proud!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, she doesn't understand a word of English.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, I thought you were Joey's other grandmother. (She just stares at him.) I've done it again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She's my biggest fan. Yeah, she's the only one in the family that's believed in me.

They both start speaking Italian to each other, and since I'm not Italian and don't understand one word, we'll move on to the English portion of the show. Not, that I'm English. Not that there's anything wrong with that, it's just that I'm not. Y'know I think I should shut-up now and move on. Anyhoo, she says something about Joey being fat I think based on this line.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I uh weighted like 27 pounds when I was born so...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from the bathroom) Hey! Happy Anniversary!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Happy Anniversary, 10 months! (They kiss.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So umm, when I was in the shower I was thinking about our first night in London...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uhh, Joey's grandmother is right there. (Points)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is that the one that speaks English or the one that doesn't?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The one that doesn't.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That was some hot love you gave me! I'm gonna go get ready.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, why don't you wear those earrings I gave you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's a great idea! I was saving them for something special.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (runs over to Phoebe) You have got to go home!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But I like it here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You gotta go home and get the earrings that you borrowed from me okay? Chandler wants me to wear them tonight.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well I think that they're in my purse. Why don't you go get dressed and I'll look for them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay! (Walks over to Rachel) Rach, hi, I need those earrings you borrowed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, umm, okay, yeah, I'll be, yeah I'll be right back. (Goes to her room.)

Phoebe then gets possessed 'cause she starts speaking in tongues. She speaks Italian to Joey's grandmother. She is quickly exorcised and returns to speaking in English. Of course, too most people English is a strange language as well. But none of them are probably reading this and if they were they wouldn't understand it. So why am I talking about them? I have absolutely no clue. Moving on...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow Pheebs, you speak Italian?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I guess so. (See, the brief possession didn't affect her at all, like we could really tell.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (She enters and hands Phoebe the earring) Here you go. Thank you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, Rach! Where's the other one?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh what, you-you want both of them?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel Karen Green, where's the other earring?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, okay, okay, look, just don't freak out, but I kinda lost it. I know it's in the apartment, but I definitely lost it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what am I going to tell Monica? She wants to wear them tonight!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Tell her to wear her own earrings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: These are her earrings.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Nooo! Nooooo! You lent me Monica's earrings?! I'm not allowed to borrow her stuff!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because I lose her stuff!

[Time Lapse, the gang is now watching Law & Order. By the way, the entire rest of the episode takes place in Monica and Rachel's apartment. Why are they called apartments when they're all stuck together? Something to think about.]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, see that blind guy right there? I'm gonna bash his head in later.
His grandmother asks him something.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh umm, my big scene is coming up. Big scene coming up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: If you said, "Big lima bean, bubbling up." Would she understand the difference?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (noticing Rachel crawling behind the couch) Rach? What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh boy, I just can't watch. It's too scary!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's a diaper commercial.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah well, you know me, babies, responsibilities, ahhh!!!
There's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Pizza delivery!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'll get it! I will get that! (Runs over and opens the door.)

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: One uh, vegetarian pizza. That's $12.15.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. Uh, by the way, if it makes you feel any better. I happen to like 8-year-old boys.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (I can't make it out.) The uh, your hair, before, your hair, you said you thought your looks like an 8-year-old's, and I'm just saying I like it. The hair.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Oh. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You understand I don't actually like 8-year-old boys.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Y'know, all I'm looking for is the money.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Here you go. (Hands her the money.) Now stop bringing us pizzas you.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: I'm gonna try. (Walks away and Ross closes the door.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You couldn't let me have her, could ya?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is a girl that I really like and had too swoop in there!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What's goin' on?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler was totally flirting with the hot delivery girl!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thank you for that! (To Monica) I was not flirting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay. I don't care. It's uh, it's fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's no big deal, I do it all the time.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (starts to laugh, but then gets serious) So umm, you-you flirt with guys all the time?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sure! It doesn't mean anything! Just like I know it doesn't mean anything with you!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but there is a big difference. You are a lot hotter than I am.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (walking past) True story! (Goes and sits down.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, this actually bothers you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, it does bother me! And I think it would bother a lot of people. Rachel, when you were going out with Ross, did it bother you when he flirted with other women?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uhh, no, no, it bothered me when he slept with other women.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And thank you, for that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But y'know, I never really had anything to worry about. Ross was never very good at the flirting thing.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? (Mumbles) What-what, what are talking about? It-it worked with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Y'know what? You're right! We meet, you flirted and then bamn nine years later you had me!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, all right. You-you-you know what I'm going to do? I am going to order another pizza and when Caitlin gets here, you-you--I will show how well I flirt. Yeah! I will, I will get her phone number! (To Chandler) And not the one on the menu!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering from Rachel's bedroom) I found it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh! Thank God! Where was it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: On your dresser.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay that is the one we already have!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, here's my big scene. My big scene's here! (They have two of the cops on the street, then they cut to where they're entering an apartment.) Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) Okay, everybody just keep smiling. It'll kill my grandmother if she finds out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (smiling) Well, what is it?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) Oh, they cut me out of the show.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (smiling) What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (smiling) Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiling) Maybe your scene's coming up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) Not likely.  'Cause you see that body bag right there

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (smiling) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) I'm in it.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, it's continued from before the commercial break.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (smiling) This is terrible, what are you going to do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) I don't know. This little, old lady lives for my career. When they dumped me off of Days of Our Lives she almost died.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (not smiling) That's not good.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smiling) No, smile! Not that face, smile! Everybody smile! (They all smile.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (smiling) Joey, why don't you just tell her what happened? It's not your fault.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (smiling) If we keep talking this way, aren't we gonna freak her out soon?

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: (Something in Italian to Joey.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Soon, soon, I'm gonna be on soon. There I am! (Points to the screen, of course it isn't him.)

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: (pointing at the screen) No! Sam Waterston!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no-no, that-that's me, that's me.

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: No, it's Sam Waterston! Crimes and Misdemeanors, Capricorn One.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Doesn't know, "Hello." But she knows Capricorn One.
Phoebe gets up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (following her) Phoebe! I have to have those earrings, we're going to leave as soon as the show is over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But I already gave them back to you!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No you didn't.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I already didn't give them back to you, that's what I said. (Walks away over to Rachel.) Where is that other earring?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's not here Pheebs, it's not here. Ohh, I went to Joey and Chandler's last night! Okay! (Goes to the door.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Make sure you check Chandler's jewelry box.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute. Chandler has a jewelry box?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, we have like ten minutes. Do you want me to get into that now?!
Rachel heads for Joey and Chandler's and Phoebe heads for the kitchen to find Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Phoebe) Hey Pheebs! How's that uh, vegetarian pizza working out for ya? You and those vegetables have a real thing going on, huh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (she just looks at him) Why are you being weird?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you like it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, that would be, "Why are you being cute?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'm working on my flirting.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohh! I did not get that.
[Cut to Chandler and Monica on the couch.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So uh Monica, do you, do you like the Law & Order?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, it's good.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: See, I'm finding out all this stuff about you today, like you like the Law & Order and that you flirted with every guy in the Tri-State area!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler! (Joey and his grandmother shush them and wave them away, which they do.) Okay, let me get this straight, it's okay for you to flirt, but not for me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I'm so glad we cleared that up. Look, I'm sorry, some things are different for men and for women.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Go on, teach me something about men and women.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I've already taught you so much already, but whatever. See when you flirt with a guy you think, "I'm just flirting, no big deal." But the guy is thinking, "Finally! Somebody who wants to sleep with me!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's true.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well that's pathetic!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Again true.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And this goes for all guys?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All guys that are awake. Then we go to sleep and then all the guys from the other end of the world wake up and behave the exact same way.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Ross in the kitchen) All right, it's another commercial; I still haven't told her!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey! This is like the last commercial. You've got like (checks his watch) ten minutes left!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know, I know! What am I going to do? (Thinks) Ooh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! You are not gonna run out and leave her here!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (dejected) Yeah, all right. (Thinks of something.) Ooh! (He quickly runs out before anyone can stop him.)

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: Joey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Joey is gonna be right back. Right back! (Tries to pantomime it for her.) Meanwhile, let's-let's-let's talk about you. (Pause.) So, you're old and small.
[Time lapse, Rachel is coming back from Chandler and Joey's.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) (To Phoebe) (whispering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Did you find it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: The earring? No. But look, I found my sunglasses under the couch! I've been looking for these since like last summer. (Puts 'em on.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, those are my sunglasses, you borrowed them from me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, calm down, here they are. (Gives back the sunglasses.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from her room) Phoebe! (Motions for the earrings. Phoebe gives her the one-minute sign.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (To Rachel) What are we going to do?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know, I don't know.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right well, we're just gonna have to tell Monica, that's all.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh gosh, she's going to kill me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I suppose I could tell her it's just all my fault.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh that'd be great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Mon, can I talk to you for a sec?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, I lost one of your earrings. I'm sorry! I am so, so sorry!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (shocked) Wow! All right well, I mean, what can you do? If you lost it you lost it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I will replace it, I promise. I feel so terrible.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, sweetie that's fine. You didn't do it on purpose.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look at you! Come here! (They hug.) Feel better?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! You're the best!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, wait a minute. Wait a minute, I-I-I, I can't do this. Listen honey, this is, it's not Phoebe's fault. She lent me the earrings, and I lost it. I'm so sorry. Honey, I feel terrible too. (Holds out her arms for a hug.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (angrily) That is exactly why I do not lend you stuff!! (Rachel looks over at Phoebe in resignation.) Okay?! I mean, first it's my jewelry! And if it's not my jewelry, it's-it's my blue sweater! And if it's not my sweater, it's my sunglasses!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Your sunglasses?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, right! (Hands Monica back her sunglasses.)

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: (from outside) Pizza!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (running to the door) Oh, mine! Mine! Mine! (to everyone) Okay, here goes. Prepare yourselves for some Class A flirting.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, hold on. (Pauses as he readies himself.) Okay. (Walks away.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, you have nothing to prove. And if you really like this girl, I don't flirting is the right thing to... (Ross interrupts and shushes her.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You'll see. Okay. (Readies himself.) Oh, what's-what's her name?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Caitlin!

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: Caitlin!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (He opens the door while faking a laugh.) Hey! Oh, we-we can't keep eating like this. (Monica turns her head in shame.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, (gets the money) so, do you make the pizzas in one of those uh, wood-burning ovens?

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: No actually umm, I think that they're umm, gas.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Gas? Wow! Intense.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) If this is the way all the Gellers flirt, we don't have a problem.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Caitlin) Hey uh, y'know that smell gas has?

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: (wanting more than anything to get the money and leave this horrible, horrible place) Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They put that in.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The gas is odorless, but they add the smell so you know when there's a leak.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: (getting more desperate to leave) Well okay!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A lot of other gas smells...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh the humanity.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Meth-methane smells...

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Y'know what umm, actually I, I really, I should go.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh but I-I-I haven't paid you yet!

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Y'know what? That's okay, you guys have ordered so many that this one is on me! (Runs for her life.)
Ross closes the door slowly.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To All) Was I talking to her about gas?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: More so than anything else.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I-I-I found it interesting.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, no-no, hey, hey, don't worry about it! In nine years, she and I will be right there. (Goes and sits on the couch dejectedly.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well, I'm gonna clear out some of these boxes. (She grabs a couple of the old pizza boxes and exits.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (walking over to Ross) Ross?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What else do they add smell too?
The street outside the building, Rachel is running to catch up with Caitlin.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey! Hey! (Stops Caitlin) Hi! Hey-hey-hey, I'm Rachel! From upstairs? The ones with all the pizza?

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Oh, is there a problem?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. No. Every thing's--they're fine. Great pizza. But it's uh, actually umm my friend Ross. He uh, just gets really nervous when he's flirting.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Oh my God! That was flirting?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Wow!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know, but uh just, I'm telling you, once, once you get past that part, that where it-it just feels like you wanna die, he's-he's really a good person.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: The guy with the gas?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. I'm-I'm telling you he's really sweet and he's really funny and he's just ugh, got a good heart. And besides, I y'know, I think he really likes you.

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well y'know, we have 7 people and like 10 pizzas, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Caitlin
lines.text: I just, I thought Joey was there.
Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe, Chandler, and Joey's grandmother are still watching Law & Order.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Hey Ross? Umm, I just ran into Caitlin in the hallway and-and uh, you must be getting better at this flirting stuff than I thought.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I don't get it, but she wanted me to give you her phone number. (Hands him the slip of paper with the phone number on it.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And she just gave you this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, thanks but uh, I don't need you doing me any favors.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I-I-I didn't! I didn't! She thought you were cute.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well that I can believe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yay!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey! Is the show still on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Almost over man.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (says hi to his grandma) Look! Oh! (Pointing out the window.) Is that the Pope?! (Chandler and his grandma turn to look and Joey slips a tape into the VCR.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why am I looking?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, here I am, here's my big scene!
Joey has made a little home movie that's his big scene. He braces himself against the door to his apartment and while holding a plastic gun and wearing the same sweater says...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on the tape) All right back off! I gotta gun! I'm not afraid to use it!

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: Oh Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's right!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You couldn't have at least changed your shirt.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on the tape) Now, I wanna a suitcase filled with 100,000 dollars. (The duck quacks, to the duck) Choo! Choo! Choo! (To the imaginary cops) Filled with $100,000 in small bills, and if I don't get it...(the duck quacks louder) Choo!! And if I don't get it, (pause, picks up the duck) I'm gonna shoot this duck!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (on the tape) I'm comin' out! (He opens the door and hops out pointing the gun in all directions and then runs out of view.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Phoebe) And she's supposed to buy this?!

lines.speaker: Grandma Tribbiani
lines.text: Joey, bravo! (Starts with that Italian stuff again.)
Suddenly, the tape cuts away from Joey's impromptu scene, to Chandler standing really, really close to the camera.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (singing) Ground control to Major Tom! Commencing countdown...engines...on!
Joey and Chandler both run to shut off the tape.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to his grandmother) That's uh, scenes from next week's show. Next week's!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I am definitely gonna watch that!
Ending Credits
Monica and Rachel's, another time lapse, Monica is seeking advice from Rachel and Phoebe about possible replacement earrings.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about these? These look the same?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Definitely!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Not as each other!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, then no.
Goes over to Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Monica) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You ready?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You look amazing. I'm the luckiest man in the world.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ohh, you're about to get a little luckier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let me see the earrings.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, honey, the earrings...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (looking at them) They look great! Does your boyfriend have the best taste or what?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: My boyfriend really does have good taste!
He turns to get his coat and Monica gives Rachel and Phoebe two thumbs up as Chandler walks over to Ross.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Ross) Thanks for picking out the earrings man.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hm-mmm.
End
